parsed_review.review_target: CP00-083 Failure Edge Recovery Secondary Workflow Binding
parsed_review.summary: Diff consumes the CP00-082 typed-primary result, binds RP00.P07.M03.S20 Hermes failure evidence plus RP00.P07.M04.S01-S14 secondary failure taxonomy/contracts/recovery expectations, completes M03+M04, and hands off to RP00.P07.M05.S01. All boundaries preserved: every runtime/write/permission/audit/LDIP flag is false, contracts are metadata_only, synthetic IDs only, and boundaryClaims are deny-all (forbidden + unknown both throw). Upstream handoff is hard-pinned (pack_id CP00-082, next_subphase RP00.P07.M03.S20, source_micro_phase_completed=false, decision=bound_metadata_only) before assertControlPlaneFailureEdgeRecoveryTypedPrimaryResult runs. assembleControlPlaneFailureEdgeRecoverySecondaryWorkflow output is deep-equal asserted against both the contract definition.result and the fixture.result (anti-theater parity), policy/result validators pin exact field sets, and the rp00 validator advances the cursor to RP00.P07.M04.S14 and gates the new subphases on control_plane_failure_edge_recovery_secondary_workflow_verified. 15 units / unit_count 15 / range and subphase_closeouts all reconcile. requested_pack_id=CP00-082 with deviation_from_plan=false is the same live-cursor-lag convention used by CP00-080/081/082, not drift. Only minor non-blocking P3 observations.

findings.title: Secondary taxonomy typed_primary_unit_id cross-refs are static metadata, not validated against the consumed CP00-082 result
findings.evidence: policies.js CONTROL_PLANE_FAILURE_EDGE_RECOVERY_SECONDARY_FAILURES embeds typed_primary_unit_id RP00.P07.M03.S02-S11; assembleControlPlaneFailureEdgeRecoverySecondaryWorkflow validates upstream pack_id/next_subphase/boundary flags and calls assertControlPlaneFailureEdgeRecoveryTypedPrimaryResult, but never asserts these typed_primary_unit_id values exist in the consumed CP00-082 failure taxonomy. IDs fall within CP00-082's covered M02.S01-M03.S19 range, so they are plausibly correct.
findings.recommended_action: No action required for closeout (metadata-only, no runtime dependency, consistent with prior packs). If the primary taxonomy is renumbered later, add a parity assertion so the cross-reference cannot silently desync.

findings.title: Minor happy-path test coverage gaps
findings.evidence: service.test.js 'assembles metadata-only secondary workflow receipt' asserts missing_matter/resource/unknown_action/etc. bound flags but skips direct assertions for missing_tenant_failure_bound and missing_actor_failure_bound (still enforced via assertControlPlaneFailureEdgeRecoverySecondaryWorkflowResult). No dedicated negative test for non-object/array upstream input or non-object boundaryClaims guards, though those guards exist in the assembler.
findings.recommended_action: Optional symmetry: add the two missing bound-flag assertions and a guard test for non-object inputs. Non-blocking; core fail-closed paths (pack drift, handoff drift, decision/boundary drift, forbidden claim, ldip claim, unknown claim) are already covered by the 7 passing tests.

raw_result.result:
I reviewed the CP00-083 diff across all eight scoped files for correctness, boundary drift, validation theater, and production_ready risk. It follows the established closeout-pack pattern with strong code↔artifact parity and genuine fail-closed gating; no boundary violations found.
